prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `4e45f183`.
codex_response:
Rule: each 5x5 tile carries one foreground motif on a shared background, and the output sorts the nine tiles into the 3x3 board by that motif’s local center-of-mass bucket: top/center/bottom crossed with left/center/right. Both summary hints were discarded: Sonnet incorrectly described vertical symmetry inside tiles, and Opus noticed the bucket structure but incorrectly said motifs slide within their original tiles rather than the tiles being reordered.

The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4e45f183/verifier.py#L4), the package wiring is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4e45f183/__init__.py#L1), the generator is in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4e45f183/generator.py#L148), and the hint correction is in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4e45f183/notes.md#L1). The generator matches the task family by keeping the fixed 19x19 separator lattice, using one global foreground/background pair, constructing canonical corner/edge/center motif prototypes with the correct buckets, and then permuting those tiles to form the input. No new shared primitive was needed.

Verification passed end to end:
- Official examples: `pass 4e45f183: 4/4 official examples passed`
- Generated set: `pass 4e45f183: 1000/1000 generated examples passed`

Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/4e45f183/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/4e45f183/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/4e45f183/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/4e45f183/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/4e45f183/previews/preview_03.png). I also checked a generated preview sheet in terminal-rendered form and the examples show the expected bucket-sorted tile layout.
